label: yes
cot_prefix: Okay, let's see. The original phrase is "in search of a midnight kiss". We need to identify which option is a humorous edit of this name. Let me look at each option: (A) in search of a midnibht kiss - This replaces "night" with "nibht". It's a small typo, but not particularly funny. Maybe a play on the word "nib" (the tip of a pen), but not very humorous. (B) in secarch of a midnight kiss - This has "secarch" instead of "search". It's just a misspelling of "search" (letters swapped), not really funny. (C) in search of a midnight piss - This replaces "kiss" with "piss". That's a humorous change because it takes a romantic phrase ("midnight kiss") and replaces it with something completely different and more crude ("midnight piss"). This would be funny because of the unexpected shift in meaning. (D) in seavrch of a midnight kiss - This has "seavrch" instead of "search". It seems like a typo with
